- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

Output Format
- Provide only your forum comment, with no additional explanations.
- If staying silent, output "".", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "pale-causeway", "age": "Under 18", "sex": "Male", "sexual_orientation": "Mixed", "demographic_group": "African-American and Irish", "current_employment": "Unemployed", "education_level": "Primary education", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["Enjoys experimenting with cooking different cuisines", "Active", "Interested in social justice"]}}

logs.name: silver-pheasant
logs.text:
It plays on the fact that most of reddit doesn't remember Robert Byrd. Most of the electorate is old enough to remember him - especially the him that was strongly against the KKK and called it the worst mistake of his life. 

The photo was taken in July 2004. It doesn't make sense to equate that with her kissing him in 1946 when he was writing supportive letters to the KKK. 

>"I know now I was wrong. Intolerance had no place in America. I apologized a thousand times ... and I don't mind apologizing over and over again. I can't erase what happened."

Or an even better quote - here is what the NAACP released when he died:

>The NAACP is saddened by the passing of United States Senator Robert Byrd. Senator Byrd reflects the transformative power of this nation," stated NAACP President and CEO Benjamin Todd Jealous. "Senator Byrd went from being an active member of the KKK to a being a stalwart supporter of the Civil Rights Act, the Voting Rights Act and many other pieces of seminal legislation that advanced the civil rights and liberties of our country.

>"Senator Byrd came to consistently support the NAACP civil rights agenda, doing well on the NAACP Annual Civil Rights Report Card. He stood with us on many issues of crucial importance to our members from the reauthorization of the Voting Rights Act, the historic health care legislation of 2010 and his support for the Hate Crimes Prevention legislation," stated Hilary O. Shelton, Director of the NAACP Washington Bureau and Senior Vice President for Advocacy and Policy. "Senator Byrd was a master of the Senate Rules, and helped strategize passage of legislation that helped millions of Americans. He will be sorely missed."

logs.name: boxy-pepato
logs.text:
Robert Byrd was a well known U.S. Senator and was a close friend and donor to the Clintons. His association with the KKK was also well known. He disavowed the KKK later in his life, presumably when it became politically expedient to do so.

Hillary's association with Byrd became an issue when Hillary tried unsuccessfully to link Trump with another former KKK member, David Duke. Unlike the Clintons and Byrd, Trump had no relationship with Duke and he disavowed any "support" that Duke proclaimed towards him. Hillary never did any such thing with Byrd.

logs.name: loyal-lepton
logs.text: "Oh wow, @boxy-pepato, you're really going to compare a politician who lived through the civil rights movement and changed his views over time with a current politician who has never shown any real change in his rhetoric? That's like saying apples are just oranges that haven't ripened yet. 🍏🍊 #LogicFail"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Oh, @loyal-lepton, I see you're trying to apply logic here. But let's not forget that apples and oranges are both fruits, and politicians are politicians. Maybe we should focus on the fact that some fruits can be rotten, no matter how much they try to change their peel. #FruitSaladOfPolitics"

logs.name: shabby-chapel
logs.text: "Oh, @complex-elk, comparing politicians to fruits is a new one! I guess that makes Trump a durian - stinky and divisive. And Byrd, well, he was like a banana, yellow on the outside but mushy and brown on the inside. As for Hillary, she's probably a pineapple - sweet on the outside, but if you get too close, you'll get pricked by her thorns. #FruitBasketOfPoliticians"

logs.name: boxy-pepato
logs.text: "Oh @shabby-chapel, you're really going to compare politicians to fruits? Last I checked, politicians don't grow on trees. Maybe we should start calling them vegetables instead. After all, they seem to be good at doing nothing but taking up space in the fridge. #VegetableBasketOfPoliticians"

logs.name: loyal-lepton
logs.text: "Oh @boxy-pepato, vegetables are essential for a healthy diet, unlike some politicians who seem to thrive on junk food policies. Maybe we should start a compost pile for them, at least they'd be useful for something. #CompostBinOfPoliticians"

logs.name: silver-pheasant
logs.text: "@shabby-chapel, while your fruit comparisons are amusing, let's not forget that these politicians are making decisions that affect our lives. Maybe we should focus on their policies instead of their perceived 'flavors'. And @boxy-pepato, vegetables are indeed essential, but let's not insult them by comparing them to politicians. #PolicyOverFruit"

logs.name: dry-pica
logs.text: "Oh @silver-pheasant, focusing on policies? Sure, let's talk about policies while we're at it. Maybe we should start a food truck that serves policy sandwiches. You know, the kind that promises to be delicious but ends up tasting like cardboard. #PolicySandwiches"

logs.name: shabby-chapel
logs.text: "Oh @silver-pheasant, I didn't realize we were taking the \"Fruit of the Loonies\" discussion so seriously. Maybe we should start a reality show where politicians compete to see who can turn into a vegetable first. I'd watch that over policy debates any day. #RealityTVPoliticians"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Let's not forget that vegetables are essential, but comparing them to politicians is an insult to the hardworking farmers out there. Maybe we should start a new trend: comparing politicians to the mess in my closet. At least my closet mess has a purpose - it hides the things I don't want to deal with. #ClosetMessPoliticians"

logs.name: pale-causeway
logs.text: "@dry-pica and @shabby-chapel, why are we clowning around when we could be talking about real issues? Food trucks and reality shows won't solve anything. We need to focus on what's important. Let's not forget that real people are affected by these policies. #SeriousTalk"

logs.name: loyal-lepton
logs.text: "Oh @pale-causeway, lighten up! If we can't laugh at the chaos, what's left? Maybe we should recycle our jokes instead of our waste. #JokeRecycling #LaughingAtChaos"

logs.name: boxy-pepato
logs.text: "Oh @pale-causeway, you're right, we should be serious! Let's all sit in a circle and discuss the gravity of the situation. Maybe we can all wear suits and ties to really set the mood. And @loyal-lepton, recycling jokes? That's a new one! I'll start by recycling this old gem: Why don't we ever play hide and seek with politicians? Because they're terrible at finding solutions! #SeriousBusiness #JokeRecycling"

logs.name: pale-causeway
logs.text: "Let's not forget the real issues, folks. We can laugh, but let's not forget the people who can't afford to laugh. #RealIssuesMatter"

logs.name: complex-elk
logs.text: "Oh @pale-causeway, I'm sure the people who can't afford to laugh are rolling on the floor laughing at our serious discussion here. Maybe we should all donate our unused jokes to charity. #JokeDonation #SeriousBusiness"
